metadata.description: Grimoire — the AI-native Skills + Agents system for the Rattle CPQ product configurator (rattleapp.de). Teaches any AI client how to turn a customer's raw pricelist, spreadsheet or ERP export into correct, BOM-aware Rattle configurator entities — and how to author CE-compliant technical documentation for the products it configures.

plugins.name: grimoire
plugins.description: Seventeen Anthropic-format skills (rattle-ingest, rattle-configurator, rattle-api, rattle-pricelist-analysis, rattle-suggest-config, rattle-bom-builder, rattle-apply-config, rattle-audit, rattle-tenant-memory, rattle-document-templates, rattle-techdoc, rattle-safety-notices, rattle-ghs-statements, rattle-techdoc-language), nine subagents (rattle-consultant, rattle-auditor, rattle-config-builder, rattle-bom-architect, rattle-techdoc-author, rattle-techdoc-auditor), eleven slash commands, six JSON Schema output contracts, golden I/O examples, and a zero-dependency Rattle MCP server that reaches every API operation through a generic passthrough — so it cannot drift when the API grows. Encodes the #1 configurator rule (explicit options for ALL variants), the raw-data → entity ingestion contract, 11 configuration rules, 4 anti-patterns, 6 structural checks, 7 idempotent ensure_* operations, the variant-BOM definition layer (usage_subclauses DSL, three option_scalings shapes, numbered options, alt_group + priority alternates, ghost depth-transparency, BOM explosion semantics), and the 15-chapter normative technical-documentation structure (DIN EN ISO 20607, IEC/IEEE 82079-1, MRL/MVO, ISO 7010, CLP/GHS).
